items.title: Do AI Agents Know When a Task Is Simple? Toward Complexity-Aware Reasoning and Execution
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly automate multi-step engineering and informatics workflows, yet they rarely ask how much effort a task actually requires. items.title: TerraZero: Procedural Driving Simulation for Zero-Demonstration Self-Play at Scale
items.summary: Training robust autonomous driving agents requires a simulator that is fast enough for reinforcement learning at scale, realistic enough to ground behavior in real-world map structure, and diverse enough to cover the safety-critical long tail that logged data rarely contains. items.title: PalmClaw: A Native On-Device Agent Framework for Mobile Phones
items.summary: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have moved beyond generating responses to executing multi-step tasks by calling tools, observing the results, and iteratively deciding the next action. Most agent systems run on desktops or servers, which support tool use and task automation. Mobile devices are also

items.title: Audio-Native Speech Recognition with a Frozen Discrete-Diffusion Language Model
items.summary: Automatic speech recognition is dominated by autoregressive decoders that emit one token at a time. We ask whether a discrete diffusion language model can transcribe speech instead, refining a whole transcript in parallel over a small number of denoising steps. items.title: Dynamic Resource Allocation for Ensemble Determinization MCTS
items.summary: Simulation-based algorithms are especially suited for high-uncertainty environments such as adversarial board games with significant elements of randomness and hidden information. items.title: Win by Silence: Deletion Non-Monotonicity, Autonomous Exploitation, and Typed-State Gating in LLM Plan Evaluation
items.summary: Plan evaluators can reward a strategic plan for becoming less explicit. This paper studies that failure in a staged expected-value scorer for LLM-generated venture routes. Proposition 1 gives the score change from deleting an interior transition while retargeting its predecessor and retaining downst

items.title: Resist and Update: Counterfactual Report Coordinates for Incentive-Compatible LLMs
items.summary: Aligned language models routinely misreport under non-evidential incentive pressure: they agree with a confident user or overstate certainty even when their internal belief is unchanged. We cast this as a failure of internal incentive-compatibility (IC) and present a method for learning and certifyi

items.title: FormalAnalyticGeo: A Neural-Symbolic Based Framework for Multimodal Analytic Geometry Problem Generation
items.summary: Math reasoning has achieved significant progress with the rapid advancement of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), however analytic geometry remains largely underexplored, primarily due to the scarcity of annotated samples. Existing diagram generation approaches struggle with analytic geometry

items.title: Form, Not Content? A Preregistered, Placebo-Controlled Evaluation of Learned Error-Conditioned Self-Repair Through Prompts and Weights in Frozen Small Code Models
items.summary: Frozen small code LLMs are deployed locally, yet the information guiding a retry after a failed attempt is still measured without placebo controls in the self-repair literature. We treat a failed program as a conjecture and an execution counterexample as an oracle-relative refutation, and introduce

items.title: ViHoRec: A Quality-Controlled Vietnamese Hotel Recommendation Dataset and Cold-Start Benchmark
items.summary: Recommender-system research for Vietnamese remains limited by the absence of a public, well-documented hotel interaction resource. Building such a resource is challenging for three reasons: cross-platform hotel names must be reconciled before interactions are comparable; quality must be audited with

items.title: Knowledge- and Gradient-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Parametrized Action Markov Decision Processes
items.summary: In this paper, we study Reinforcement Learning in Parametrized Action Markov Decision Processes (PAMDP), where each decision consists of a symbolic action and numerical parameters. In such settings Reinforcement Learning algorithms typically determine parameters with one-shot estimators, which makes

items.title: Real-time fall detection based on vision for low-power edge platforms
items.summary: Falling detection is vital for elderly care and intelligent surveillance; however, prevailing vision-based approaches predominantly frame it as static pose classification or discrete temporal pattern matching, fundamentally overlooking the instability dynamics of the human support system. items.title: MemOps: Benchmarking Lifecycle Memory Operations in Long-Horizon Conversations
items.summary: Long-term memory has become a foundational capability for LLM-based agents that accompany users across extended, multi-session interactions. Existing benchmarks, however, evaluate such memory almost exclusively through downstream question answering, scoring only the correctness of a final answer. Th

items.title: UR-VC: Unsupervised Robotic Value Correction for Time-Derived Progress Proxies
items.summary: Modern robot learning systems increasingly rely on dense progress or value signals to evaluate intermediate states, guide policy learning, and detect task completion, making the quality of these signals critical. Since such dense labels are rarely available at scale, normalized time within a demonst

items.title: A Multi-Agent System for Autonomous, Fine-Tuning-Free Clinical Symptom Detection: Development and Validation Study
items.summary: Clinical notes contain many of the signs and symptoms that bring patients to care, yet this information rarely reaches structured fields. Existing extraction approaches either rely on context-insensitive rules that generate false positives or on supervised models that require substantial fine-tuning

items.title: Unveiling Complex Collective Behaviors from Simple Rewards
items.summary: Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) holds great potential for robot swarms, but the black-box nature of neural policies complicates strategic analysis, limiting multi-robot applications. Furthermore, complex swarm behaviors can surprisingly emerge from simple rewards without explicit aggregati

items.title: ChartGenEval: Corruption-Tested Multi-Dimensional Feedback for Rhythm-Game Chart Generation
items.summary: A generated rhythm-game chart need not reproduce one official note sequence: many note choices can fit the same song and difficulty. Reference-note agreement therefore measures reconstruction, not the full design problem. We introduce ChartGenEval, a six-question evaluation framework with an automat

items.title: Reproducible Reservoir Computing with Thermally Driven Superparamagnets: Controlling Temperature Sensitivity
items.summary: Unconventional computing systems must demonstrate robust performance under real-world environmental conditions to enable practical deployments. We have recently proposed superparamagnetic nanodot ensembles driven by strain-induced magnetoelectric coupling as exciting candidates for use as ultra-low

items.title: Accelerating Masked Diffusion Large Language Models: A Survey of Efficient Inference Techniques
items.summary: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) offer a theoretical advantage in parallel generation over standard autoregressive models. However, parallel generation alone does not guarantee practical speedups. Realizing this efficiency requires specialized inference mechanisms, such as diffusion-aware cac

items.title: Solution of the Hempel's statistical ambiguity problem and Causal AI
items.summary: This paper addresses Carl Hempel's longstanding problem of statistical ambiguity in inductive-statistical inference, in which contradictory predictions are derived from statistical laws. To avoid such predictions, Carl Hempel proposed the Requirement of Maximal Specificity (RMS) for the statistical

items.title: The Seriality Gap in Video Diffusion Models
items.summary: When one ball strikes another, then another, video models should predict the consequences of each bounce. In controlled experiments on multi-ball hard-sphere dynamics, we find that the performance of standard bidirectional video diffusion degrades as the causal chain lengthens, even when provided mo

items.title: A Shortcut to Statistically Steady-State Turbulence with Flow Matching
items.summary: Many nonlinear physical systems exhibit an initial transient phase in which perturbations grow before nonlinear interactions lead to a statistically steady state. While this saturated regime is of primary interest, direct numerical simulations must resolve the full transient dynamics before reaching

items.title: The Spectrum Is Not Enough: When Context Helps Time-Series Forecasting
items.summary: A growing family of indices scores how predictable a series is from its spectrum. Practitioners increasingly read these scores as answering a different question: whether \emph{adding context}, a longer lookback, a retrieval plug-in, or a pretrained model, will help. These are not the same question.

items.title: Watermark Forensics for Generative Models: An Information-Theoretic Perspective
items.summary: A watermark in a generative model's output is usually asked only whether a text is machine-made. The same mark can do more: attribute it to the user who produced it, extract a hidden payload, or localize the part that survives editing. These form a forensic ladder, and we ask what each rung costs in

items.title: Ensemble Controlled-Flow Filtering for Implicit Data Assimilation
items.summary: Data assimilation estimates the state of a dynamical system from model forecasts and incoming observations. items.title: Robustness of Deep Learning Models for PV Power Forecasting under NWP Forecast Errors: A Spatiotemporal and Physically Interpretable Analysis
items.summary: Engineering use of AI forecasting models requires not only high nominal accuracy but also predictable behavior under uncertain inputs. items.title: Efficient Sequential Calibration with $O(T^{2/3-ε})$ Error Bound
items.summary: We study the online binary sequential calibration problem. A recent breakthrough by \citet{dagan2024breaking} overcomes the classical \(T^{2/3}\) barrier for calibration error. items.title: LatentFlow: A General Framework for Conditioning Stochastic Processes
items.summary: Stochastic-process models are, as a rule, far easier to simulate than to condition.
